description_long: This is a rounded, double-handled ceramic vase with a wide, bulbous body and a short, cylindrical neck. The surface is matte and displays a natural, earthy color palette with gradients of terracotta, beige, and darkened patches, suggesting age or exposure to elements. The handles are small, stubby, and positioned symmetrically on opposite sides of the body, blending smoothly into the vessel. The rim is thick and slightly uneven, giving the vase a handcrafted appearance. The interior is visible and appears hollow, suitable for holding water or decorative items. The overall form and finish evoke a rustic, utilitarian style often found in traditional or historical urban settings.
description: A rustic, rounded ceramic vase with two small side handles, earthy tones, and a matte, weathered finish. The thick rim and bulbous body give it a handcrafted, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded body, thick rim, and both side handles visible, with a warm terracotta color and subtle dark patches.
description_view_1: Side view highlights one handle prominently, with the other obscured, and more pronounced dark discoloration on the lower body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirrors the front, with both handles visible and a slightly lighter surface coloration.
description_view_3: Rear view centers on the second handle, with the body showing more darkened, weathered areas and the rim slightly uneven.
